events.text: Patient is a 62-year-old African American male, height 5 feet 10 inches, weight 195 pounds, ECOG performance status 1.

At age 62, patient presented with progressive low back pain and fatigue over 3 months; laboratory workup revealed hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL, calcium 11.2 mg/dL, and beta-2 microglobulin 6.8 mg/L with albumin 3.2 g/dL consistent with ISS Stage III multiple myeloma.

At age 62, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 65% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138, CD56, and cytoplasmic IgG kappa; Congo red stain negative for amyloid.

At age 62, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate demonstrated gain(1q21) in 45% of plasma cells; no del(17p), t(4;14), or t(14;16) detected; overall cytogenetic risk classified as intermediate; R-ISS Stage III based on ISS III plus elevated LDH at 280 U/L.

At age 62, serum protein electrophoresis demonstrated an M-spike of 4.2 g/dL in the gamma region; immunofixation confirmed IgG kappa monoclonal protein; serum free light chain kappa 185 mg/L, lambda 12 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 15.4; 24-hour urine showed Bence Jones proteinuria at 1.8 g/day.

At age 62, whole-body low-dose CT revealed multiple lytic lesions involving the skull, bilateral humeri, thoracic and lumbar spine, pelvis, and bilateral femora; largest lesion measuring 3.2 cm in the left iliac bone with associated soft tissue component.
At age 62, MRI of the thoracolumbar spine showed diffuse marrow infiltration with multiple compression fractures at T8, T11, and L2; no epidural extension or spinal cord compression identified.

At age 62, patient started on VRd induction therapy consisting of bortezomib 1.3 mg/m2 subcutaneously on days 1, 4, 8, and 11, lenalidomide 25 mg orally days 1 through 14, and dexamethasone 40 mg orally on days 1, 2, 4, 5, 8, 9, 11, and 12 on a 21-day cycle; also started on acyclovir 400 mg twice daily for varicella prophylaxis and aspirin 325 mg daily for DVT prophylaxis.
At age 62, cycle 1 of VRd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction was administered; patient tolerated treatment with grade 1 nausea and mild constipation.

At age 62, after cycle 2 of VRd, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and tingling in bilateral feet; bortezomib dose reduced to 1.0 mg/m2 and changed to weekly dosing schedule.

At age 62, cycles 3 and 4 of VRd completed with dose-modified bortezomib; repeat SPEP showed M-spike decreased to 1.1 g/dL; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 42 mg/L; assessment consistent with very good partial response per IMWG criteria.

At age 62, after 4 cycles of VRd, restaging labs showed hemoglobin improved to 11.4 g/dL, creatinine normalized to 1.1 mg/dL, calcium 9.6 mg/dL, LDH 195 U/L, beta-2 microglobulin decreased to 3.1 mg/L; patient deemed eligible for autologous stem cell transplant.

At age 62, patient underwent stem cell mobilization with cyclophosphamide 2 g/m2 followed by G-CSF 10 mcg/kg daily; adequate CD34-positive stem cells collected at 5.2 million cells per kilogram over 2 days of apheresis.

At age 62, patient received high-dose melphalan conditioning at 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant; engraftment achieved on day plus 12 with ANC greater than 500 and platelet count greater than 20,000 by day plus 16.

At age 62, during post-transplant recovery, patient developed grade 3 mucositis requiring IV narcotics and total parenteral nutrition for 5 days; also had febrile neutropenia managed with broad-spectrum antibiotics.

At age 63, day plus 100 post-transplant assessment showed SPEP with no detectable M-spike; immunofixation negative; serum free light chain ratio normalized at 1.4; bone marrow biopsy showed less than 5% plasma cells; response classified as complete response per IMWG criteria.

At age 63, bone marrow biopsy at day plus 100 demonstrated 3% plasma cells by morphology with no aberrant phenotype on flow cytometry; MRD assessment by multiparameter flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity was negative.
At age 63, patient started lenalidomide maintenance therapy at 10 mg orally days 1 through 21 of a 28-day cycle; CBC monitored monthly with dose adjustments planned for cytopenias.
At age 63, after 4 months of lenalidomide maintenance, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia with ANC 0.9; lenalidomide held for 1 week then resumed at 5 mg daily.
At age 64, annual PET/CT showed no FDG-avid lesions; previously noted lytic lesions stable with sclerotic changes indicating healing response; no new osseous or extramedullary disease.

At age 64, annual restaging labs showed SPEP with no M-spike, immunofixation negative, serum free light chain ratio 1.2, hemoglobin 13.1 g/dL, creatinine 1.0 mg/dL; maintained in complete response on lenalidomide maintenance.

At age 65, routine monitoring showed persistent complete response; lenalidomide maintenance continued at 5 mg daily; peripheral neuropathy improved to grade 1 with residual mild numbness in toes; ECOG performance status 0.

At age 65, patient developed herpes zoster reactivation on the left T10 dermatome despite acyclovir prophylaxis; treated with valacyclovir 1 g three times daily for 7 days with resolution; acyclovir prophylaxis dose increased.

At age 66, after 3 years of lenalidomide maintenance, SPEP showed re-emergence of M-spike at 0.6 g/dL; serum free kappa light chain rising to 68 mg/L with ratio of 8.5; biochemical relapse confirmed per IMWG criteria.
At age 66, PET/CT at relapse showed new FDG-avid lesion in the right femoral shaft and increased uptake in the L4 vertebral body; no extramedullary plasmacytomas identified.

At age 66, patient started daratumumab-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (DRd) salvage regimen with daratumumab 16 mg/kg IV weekly for cycles 1 and 2, then biweekly, lenalidomide 25 mg days 1 through 21, and dexamethasone 40 mg weekly on a 28-day cycle.

At age 66, during first daratumumab infusion, patient experienced grade 2 infusion-related reaction with chills, nasal congestion, and mild throat tightness; managed with IV diphenhydramine and acetaminophen; subsequent infusions tolerated with premedication.
At age 66, patient received palliative external beam radiation therapy to the right femoral shaft lesion, 30 Gy delivered in 10 fractions over 2 weeks for pain control and fracture prevention.

At age 67, after 6 cycles of DRd, SPEP showed M-spike decreased to undetectable level; serum free light chain ratio normalized; assessment classified as very good partial response trending toward complete response; treatment continued with daratumumab every 4 weeks.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 62, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 65% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138, CD56, and cytoplasmic IgG kappa; Congo red stain negative for amyloid.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

At age 62, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate demonstrated gain(1q21) in 45% of plasma cells; no del(17p), t(4;14), or t(14;16) detected; overall cytogenetic risk classified as intermediate; R-ISS Stage III based on ISS III plus elevated LDH at 280 U/L.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Multiple myeloma, ISS stage II-III. IgG kappa or IgA lambda subtypes. Treated with VRd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction followed by autologous stem cell transplant and lenalidomide maintenance. Include serum protein electrophoresis, free light chains, beta-2 microglobulin, FISH for del(17p) and t(4;14).
